name: opencode-agent
description: Native chat agent powered by the opencode CLI.

commands.title: Add Terminal Selection to opencode Chat

enumDescriptions: Prompt before opencode applies each file edit (the pending diff is shown in chat first).
Apply file edits immediately; review them afterwards via the Keep/Undo pills.
opencode.editApproval.description: Whether opencode must ask for approval before editing files. Edits are blocked until you respond; "Allow for Session" stops prompting until the window is reloaded.

enumDescriptions: Prompt before opencode runs each shell command (the command is shown in chat first).
opencode.commandApproval.description: Whether opencode must ask for approval before running shell (bash) commands. Commands are blocked until you respond; "Allow for Session" stops prompting until the window is reloaded. Applies to new chat sessions.

enumDescriptions: Use opencode's own LLM-generated session title. If opencode's title generation is disabled or times out, fall back to a title derived from your first message.
Always derive the session title from your first message (no extra model call), ignoring opencode's title generator.
Never set a session title; keep opencode's placeholder ("New session - <timestamp>").
opencode.sessionTitle.markdownDescription: How new chat sessions are titled. `auto` waits for opencode's own title, then falls back to your first message; `local` always derives the title from your first message; `off` keeps opencode's placeholder.

opencode.suggestFollowups.description: Show suggested follow-up chips under each response. Suggestions are derived from what the turn did (e.g. reviewing or testing edits) — no extra model call.

opencode.inlineCompletions.enabled.description: Show ghost-text inline completions (Tab to accept) from a fill-in-the-middle model server. If the server is unreachable, completions back off automatically and never block typing.

opencode.inlineCompletions.endpoint.markdownDescription: URL of the completion server. For `openai` API mode this is an OpenAI-compatible `/v1/completions` endpoint (uses the `suffix` field for fill-in-the-middle); for `llama` mode it's llama.cpp's `/infill` endpoint.

opencode.inlineCompletions.api.description: Request/response shape the completion endpoint expects.

opencode.inlineCompletions.model.description: Model name to send with completion requests (optional; many local servers ignore it).

opencode.inlineCompletions.maxTokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to request per inline completion.

opencode.inlineCompletions.disabledLangu.markdownDescription: Language ids to disable inline completions for (e.g. `["markdown", "plaintext"]`). Accept the next word with `Cmd/Ctrl+→` and the next line with the built-in `editor.action.inlineSuggest.acceptNextLine` command.

opencode.systemPrompt.markdownDescription: Extra system instructions sent with every opencode turn. Appended **after** opencode's built-in agent prompt (it augments, never replaces it). Also editable from chat via `/system`.

opencode.commitMessageModel.markdownDescription: OpenRouter model slug used by **Generate Commit Message** in the Source Control input (e.g. `anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5` or another cheap, fast model). Authenticates with the `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` environment variable — the same one opencode's OpenRouter provider uses.

chatParticipants.name: opencode
chatParticipants.description: Ask opencode to build, edit, or explain code

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Run opencode in read-only Plan mode

commands.name: system
commands.description: Edit extra system instructions in a markdown editor (/system <text> sets inline, /system clear)

commands.name: usage
commands.description: Show context, token, and cost usage for this session
